outcome_definition.outcome: fail = 1 if results=='fail' else 0, restricted to substantive inspections

leakage_flags.evidence: free-text list of cited violations; present for 70.3% of rows; present in 97.4% of fails vs 70.5% of passes; mechanically generated by the inspection result.
identical to results in 78.5% of rows; it IS the outcome.
mean violation_count = 7.3 for fails vs 2.6 for non-fails; it is a downstream count of citations, i.e. an alternative outcome, NOT a covariate.
leakage_flags.action: USE ONLY as an alternative (secondary) outcome, never as a regressor.

leakage_flags.evidence: daily weather keyed to inspection date; plausibly unrelated to a given establishment's latent food-safety conditions; shared by all inspections on the same calendar day.
leakage_flags.action: Eligible RHS / treatment, with seasonality + date controls and day-clustered SE.

weather_variation.note: weather is identical for all inspections sharing a calendar date (within-day std=0) -> treatment varies only across days; MUST cluster SE by date (effective N = n_days, not n_inspections).

repeat_structure.note: establishment FE feasible: most inspections are in repeatedly-inspected establishments; within-establishment weather variation across inspection dates identifies a weather effect net of fixed location/cuisine/management quality.

supported_designs.design: Cross-sectional / pooled OLS-LPM (or logit): fail ~ weather + seasonality + zip + facility/risk FE
supported_designs.identification: weather-on-inspection-day as-good-as-random conditional on season/date; exogeneity of weather to latent establishment conditions.
supported_designs.diagnostic: day-clustered SE; seasonality robustness (month-of-year & DOY controls); placebo with NEXT-period weather.

supported_designs.design: Establishment fixed-effects LPM: within-estab variation in weather across inspection dates
supported_designs.identification: compares the SAME establishment inspected on hot vs cold/wet days; removes all time-invariant confounders.
supported_designs.diagnostic: two-way (estab + month) FE; cluster by date; test sensitivity to inspection_type composition.

supported_designs.design: Inspector/route-style heat effect on leniency (violation_count as secondary outcome)
supported_designs.diagnostic: poisson/NB; same controls; report as secondary.

supported_designs.design: RDD on a temperature threshold (e.g. freezing 0C)
supported_designs.identification: no policy/behavioral discontinuity at a known temperature cutoff; weather is continuous with no assignment rule.
supported_designs.diagnostic: n/a — REJECTED, no credible running-variable cutoff.

supported_designs.identification: no discrete treatment turning on at a date for a treated subgroup; weather is continuous and universal.

recommended_primary: Establishment-FE linear probability model of inspection FAIL on inspection-day temperature (and precipitation), with month FE and SE clustered by calendar date; pooled LPM with season/zip/facility controls as the headline if FE sample too thin.
